title: Application Profiling Quiz
description: Test your knowledge about the critical role of application profiling and performance tuning in modern web applications.

questions.question: What is the primary role of 'Application Profiling' in a web application?
options: To store all of the application's users.
To analyze exactly how your application uses resources (like CPU, memory, and the event loop) to identify performance bottlenecks.
To automatically generate a backup of the data.
To hide the application's IP address.

questions.question: Which of these flags should you use when starting Node.js locally to use Chrome DevTools for profiling?
What is a 'Heap Snapshot' in the context of memory profiling?
options: A way to encrypt files using a secret key.
A record of all the objects in your application's memory at a single point in time, useful for identifying memory leaks.
A way to automatically compress files.

questions.question: Which of these is a popular open-source suite for profiling and monitoring Node.js applications?
What happens when a single-threaded Node.js application executes a long-running synchronous function?
options: It blocks the event loop, preventing your server from handling any other incoming requests until the function is finished.
To automatically generate a backup of the data.
